revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''豊橋祇園祭'''(とよはしぎおんまつり) 豊橋祇園祭(とよはしぎおんまつり)は豊橋市にある吉田神社の祭事。 ==概要== 例...'
revision.text:
'''豊橋祇園祭'''(とよはしぎおんまつり) 豊橋祇園祭(とよはしぎおんまつり)は豊橋市にある吉田神社の祭事。
==概要== 例年7月中旬に行われる。神輿渡御を本祭とし、前々夜の手筒花火、前夜の打ち上げ花火も有名である。
==由来・歴史== 江戸時代に天王祭として行われ、山本貞晨著作『三河国吉田名跡綜録』という絵文書にもその様子が描かれている(吉田神社所蔵)。神輿渡御の行列は源頼朝をしのぶものとされ、鎌倉・平安時代の服装で練り歩く。 なお手筒花火は永禄元年(1558)に今川義元の吉田城代、大原備前守の吉田神社への奉納に始まるとされている。吉田神社内に『伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地』の碑がある。大筒花火は町内会が自分で手作り、それをみこしがわりに担いで練り歩く。
==日程== 前日(木曜日) 子供笹踊り:午後3時～5時 初日(金曜日) 宵祭り:午後6時より 神前花火奉納(手筒花火):午後6時45分より 二日目(土曜日) 前夜祭(打上花火):午後6時より 最終日(日曜日) 例祭式典:午後10時より献幣使参向・浦安の舞奉納 御幸祭:午後5時より神輿渡御出発
==関連項目== [[祇園祭]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.tees.ne.jp/~toyo-gion/ 豊橋祇園祭奉賛会ホームページ(公式サイト)] *[http://www.bubbles.jp/gion/shiryou.html 豊橋祇園祭資料室(豊橋祇園祭奉賛会)]

'''豊橋祇園祭'''（とよはしぎおんまつり）は、[[豊橋市]]にある[[吉田神社]]の祭事。
==概要== 例年[[7月]]中旬に行われる。[[神輿渡御]]を本祭とし、前々夜の[[手筒花火]]、前夜の[[打ち上げ花火]]も有名である。
==由来・歴史== [[江戸時代]]に天王祭として行われ、[[山本貞晨]]著作『三河国吉田名跡綜録』（吉田神社所蔵）という絵文書にもその様子が描かれている。神輿渡御の行列は[[源頼朝]]をしのぶものとされ、[[鎌倉時代|鎌倉]]・[[平安時代]]の服装で練り歩く。
なお手筒花火は[[永禄]]元年（[[1558年]]）に[[今川義元]]の[[吉田城]]代、[[大原備前守]]の吉田神社への奉納に始まるとされている。吉田神社内に『伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地』の碑がある。大筒花火は町内会が自分で手作り、それを神輿がわりに担いで練り歩く。
==日程== ===前日（木曜日）=== *子供笹踊り：午後3時～5時 ===初日（金曜日）=== *宵祭り：午後6時より *神前花火奉納（手筒花火）：午後6時45分より ===二日目（土曜日）=== *前夜祭（打ち上げ花火）：午後6時より ===最終日（日曜日）=== *例祭式典：午後10時より[[献幣使]]参向、[[浦安の舞]]奉納 *御幸祭：午後5時より神輿渡御出発
==関連項目== *[[祇園祭]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.tees.ne.jp/~toyo-gion/ 豊橋祇園祭奉賛会ホームページ(公式サイト)] *[http://www.bubbles.jp/gion/shiryou.html 豊橋祇園祭資料室(豊橋祇園祭奉賛会)]

'''豊橋祇園祭'''（とよはしぎおんまつり）は、[[豊橋市]]にある[[吉田神社]]の祭事。
==概要== 例年[[7月]]中旬に行われる。[[神輿渡御]]を本祭とし、前々夜の[[手筒花火]]、前夜の[[打ち上げ花火]]も有名である。
==由来・歴史== [[江戸時代]]に天王祭として行われ、[[山本貞晨]]著作『三河国吉田名跡綜録』（吉田神社所蔵）という絵文書にもその様子が描かれている。神輿渡御の行列は[[源頼朝]]をしのぶものとされ、[[鎌倉時代|鎌倉]]・[[平安時代]]の服装で練り歩く。
なお手筒花火は[[永禄]]元年（[[1558年]]）に[[今川義元]]の[[吉田城]]代、[[大原備前守]]の吉田神社への奉納に始まるとされている。吉田神社内に『伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地』の碑がある。大筒花火は町内会が自分で手作り、それを神輿がわりに担いで練り歩く。
==日程== ===前日（木曜日）=== *子供笹踊り：午後3時～5時 ===初日（金曜日）=== *宵祭り：午後6時より *神前花火奉納（手筒花火）：午後6時45分より ===二日目（土曜日）=== *前夜祭（打ち上げ花火）：午後6時より ===最終日（日曜日）=== *例祭式典：午後10時より[[献幣使]]参向、[[浦安の舞]]奉納 *御幸祭：午後5時より神輿渡御出発
==関連項目== *[[祇園祭]]

revision.comment: 同名で全く別の吉田神社へのリンクになっており、単独記事が存在しないのでリンクを暫定的に外す

'''豊橋祇園祭'''（とよはしぎおんまつり）は、[[豊橋市]]にある吉田神社の祭事。
==概要== 例年[[7月]]中旬に行われる。[[神輿渡御]]を本祭とし、前々夜の[[手筒花火]]、前夜の[[打ち上げ花火]]も有名である。
==由来・歴史== [[江戸時代]]に天王祭として行われ、[[山本貞晨]]著作『三河国吉田名跡綜録』（吉田神社所蔵）という絵文書にもその様子が描かれている。神輿渡御の行列は[[源頼朝]]をしのぶものとされ、[[鎌倉時代|鎌倉]]・[[平安時代]]の服装で練り歩く。
なお手筒花火は[[永禄]]元年（[[1558年]]）に[[今川義元]]の[[吉田城 (三河国)|吉田城]]代、[[小原鎮実|大原備前守]]の吉田神社への奉納に始まるとされている。吉田神社内に『伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地』の碑がある。大筒花火は町内会が自分で手作り、それを神輿がわりに担いで練り歩く。
==日程== [[ファイル:TezutsuHanabi YoshidaJinja.jpg|thumb|250px|手筒花火放揚 最後のハネ爆発]] *前日(木曜日) **子供笹踊り：午後3時～5時 *初日(金曜日) **宵祭り：午後6時より **神前花火奉納（手筒花火）：午後6時45分より *二日目(土曜日) **前夜祭（打ち上げ花火）：午後6時より *最終日(日曜日) **例祭式典：午後10時より[[献幣使]]参向、[[浦安の舞]]奉納 **御幸祭：午後5時より神輿渡御出発
==関連項目== *[[祇園祭]]
==外部リンク== *[http://www.tees.ne.jp/~toyo-gion/ 豊橋祇園祭奉賛会公式サイト] *[http://www.bubbles.jp/gion/shiryou.html 豊橋祇園祭資料室(豊橋祇園祭奉賛会)]

なお手筒花火は[[永禄]]元年（[[1558年]]）に[[今川義元]]の[[吉田城 (三河国)|吉田城]]代、[[小原鎮実|大原備前守]]の吉田神社への奉納に始まるとされている。吉田神社内に『伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地』の碑がある。大筒花火は町内会が自分で手作り、それを神輿がわりに担いで練り歩く。

==日程== *前日（木曜日） **子供笹踊り：15〜17時 *初日（金曜日） **宵祭り：18時より **神前花火奉納（手筒花火）：18時45分より *二日目（土曜日） **前夜祭（打ち上げ花火）：18時より *最終日（日曜日） **例祭式典：10時より[[献幣使]]参向、[[浦安の舞]]奉納 **御幸祭：17時より神輿渡御出発
==関連項目== *[[祇園祭]] *[[吉田神社 (豊橋市)]]
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} *[https://www.toyohashigion.org/ 豊橋祇園祭奉賛会公式サイト] *[http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 豊橋の煙火見物] 『旅の小遣帳』時事新報家庭部 編（正和堂書房、1930）

[[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|豊橋祇園祭・打ち上げ花火]] '''豊橋祇園祭'''（とよはしぎおんまつり）は、[[豊橋市]][[関屋|関屋町]]の[[吉田神社 (豊橋市)|吉田神社]]で毎年7月中旬に行われる祭事である。
[[神輿渡御]]を本祭とする。前々夜の[[手筒花火]]、前夜の打ち上げ[[花火]]も有名である。
==由来・歴史== [[ファイル:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|サムネイル|天王祭。3代広重作]] [[江戸時代]]に天王祭として行われ、[[山本貞晨]]著作『三河国吉田名跡綜録』（吉田神社所蔵）という絵文書にもその様子が描かれている。神輿渡御の行列は[[源頼朝]]をしのぶものとされ、[[鎌倉時代|鎌倉]]・[[平安時代]]の服装で練り歩く。
なお手筒花火は[[永禄]]元年（[[1558年]]）に[[今川義元]]の[[吉田城 (三河国)|吉田城]]代、[[小原鎮実|大原備前守]]の吉田神社への奉納に始まるとされている。吉田神社内に「伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地」の碑がある。大筒花火は町内会が自分で手作り、それを神輿がわりに担いで練り歩く。
==日程== *前日（木曜日） **子供笹踊り：15〜17時 *初日（金曜日） **宵祭り：18時より **神前花火奉納（手筒花火）：18時45分より *二日目（土曜日） **前夜祭（打ち上げ花火）：18時より *最終日（日曜日） **例祭式典：10時より[[献幣使]]参向、[[浦安の舞]]奉納 **御幸祭：17時より神輿渡御出発
==関連項目== *[[祇園祭]] *[[吉田神社 (豊橋市)]]
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} *[https://www.toyohashigion.org/ 豊橋祇園祭奉賛会公式サイト] *[http://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 豊橋の煙火見物] 『旅の小遣帳』時事新報家庭部 編（正和堂書房、1930）

[[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|Toyohashi Gion Festival – Fireworks Display]] '''Toyohashi Gion Festival''' (豊橋祇園祭) is a festival held every **mid-July** at {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)}} in **Sekiwa Town (関屋町), Toyohashi City (豊橋市)**.
The festival centers around the **Mikoshi Togyo (神輿渡御, portable shrine procession)**. Additionally, the **Tezutsu Fireworks (手筒花火)** on the night before the festival and the **fireworks display** on the eve of the main event are well known.
== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by {{ill|Utagawa Hiroshige|ja|歌川広重}}]] The festival **originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)**. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript **"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)**, authored by **{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨}}**, which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The **mikoshi procession** is said to **commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)**, with participants **parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire**.
Additionally, **Tezutsu Fireworks** are believed to have begun in **Eiroku 1 (1558)** when **{{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元}}’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine**. A stone monument **"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)** stands within the shrine grounds. The **Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)** are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they **parade them like mikoshi**.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * **Day Before (Thursday)** * Children's **Sasa Dance (笹踊り)**: 15:00–17:00
* **First Day (Friday)** * **Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)**: From 18:00 * **Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)**: From 18:45
* **Second Day (Saturday)** * **Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)**: From 18:00
* **Final Day (Sunday)** * **Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)**: From 10:00 – Includes the **Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)** and **Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance** * **Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)**: From 17:00
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Gion Festival|ja|祇園祭}} * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)
{{Event-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}}

== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by {{ill|Utagawa Hiroshige|ja|歌川広重}}]] The festival **originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)**. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript **"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)**, authored by **{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨}}**, which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The **mikoshi procession** is said to **commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)**, with participants **parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire**.
Additionally, **Tezutsu Fireworks** are believed to have begun in **Eiroku 1 (1558)** when **{{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元}}’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine**. A stone monument **"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)** stands within the shrine grounds. The **Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)** are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they **parade them like mikoshi**.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * **Day Before (Thursday)** * Children's **Sasa Dance (笹踊り)**: 15:00–17:00
* **First Day (Friday)** * **Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)**: From 18:00 * **Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)**: From 18:45
* **Second Day (Saturday)** * **Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)**: From 18:00
* **Final Day (Sunday)** * **Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)**: From 10:00 – Includes the **Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)** and **Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance** * **Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)**: From 17:00
== See Also == * {{ill|Gion Festival|ja|祇園祭}} * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)

[[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|Toyohashi Gion Festival – Fireworks Display]] '''Toyohashi Gion Festival''' (豊橋祇園祭) is a festival held every **mid-July** at {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)}} in **Sekiwa Town (関屋町), Toyohashi City (豊橋市)**.
The festival centers around the **Mikoshi Togyo (神輿渡御, portable shrine procession)**. Additionally, the **Tezutsu Fireworks (手筒花火)** on the night before the festival and the **fireworks display** on the eve of the main event are well known.
== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by {{ill|Utagawa Hiroshige|ja|歌川広重}}]] The festival **originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)**. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript **"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)**, authored by **{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨}}**, which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The **mikoshi procession** is said to **commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)**, with participants **parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire**.
Additionally, **Tezutsu Fireworks** are believed to have begun in **Eiroku 1 (1558)** when **{{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元}}’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine**. A stone monument **"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)** stands within the shrine grounds. The **Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)** are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they **parade them like mikoshi**.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * **Day Before (Thursday)** * Children's **Sasa Dance (笹踊り)**: 15:00–17:00
* **First Day (Friday)** * **Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)**: From 18:00 * **Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)**: From 18:45
* **Second Day (Saturday)** * **Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)**: From 18:00
* **Final Day (Sunday)** * **Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)**: From 10:00 – Includes the **Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)** and **Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance** * **Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)**: From 17:00
== See Also == * {{ill|Gion Festival|ja|祇園祭}} * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)
{{Event-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}}

[[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|Toyohashi Gion Festival – Fireworks Display]] '''Toyohashi Gion Festival''' (豊橋祇園祭) is a festival held every **mid-July** at {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)}} in **Sekiwa Town (関屋町), Toyohashi City (豊橋市)**.
The festival centers around the **Mikoshi Togyo (神輿渡御, portable shrine procession)**. Additionally, the **Tezutsu Fireworks (手筒花火)** on the night before the festival and the **fireworks display** on the eve of the main event are well known.
== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by {{ill|Utagawa Hiroshige|ja|歌川広重}}]] The festival **originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)**. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript **"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)**, authored by **{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨}}**, which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The **mikoshi procession** is said to **commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)**, with participants **parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire**.
Additionally, **Tezutsu Fireworks** are believed to have begun in **Eiroku 1 (1558)** when **{{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元}}’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine**. A stone monument **"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)** stands within the shrine grounds. The **Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)** are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they **parade them like mikoshi**.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * **Day Before (Thursday)** * Children's **Sasa Dance (笹踊り)**: 15:00–17:00
* **First Day (Friday)** * **Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)**: From 18:00 * **Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)**: From 18:45
* **Second Day (Saturday)** * **Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)**: From 18:00
* **Final Day (Sunday)** * **Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)**: From 10:00 – Includes the **Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)** and **Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance** * **Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)**: From 17:00
== See Also == * {{ill|Gion Festival|ja|祇園祭}} * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)
{{Event-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}}

The festival centers around the **Mikoshi Togyo (神輿渡御, portable shrine procession)**. Additionally, the **Tezutsu Fireworks (手筒花火)** on the night before the festival and the **fireworks display** on the eve of the main event are well known.
== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by {{ill|Utagawa Hiroshige|ja|歌川広重}}]] The festival **originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)**. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript **"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)**, authored by **{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨}}**, which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The **mikoshi procession** is said to **commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)**, with participants **parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire**.
Additionally, **Tezutsu Fireworks** are believed to have begun in **Eiroku 1 (1558)** when **{{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元}}’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine**. A stone monument **"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)** stands within the shrine grounds. The **Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)** are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they **parade them like mikoshi**.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * **Day Before (Thursday)** * Children's **Sasa Dance (笹踊り)**: 15:00–17:00
* **First Day (Friday)** * **Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)**: From 18:00 * **Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)**: From 18:45
* **Second Day (Saturday)** * **Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)**: From 18:00
* **Final Day (Sunday)** * **Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)**: From 10:00 – Includes the **Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)** and **Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance** * **Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)**: From 17:00
== See Also == * {{ill|Gion Festival|ja|祇園祭}} * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)
{{Event-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}}

It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript **"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)**, authored by **{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨}}**, which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The **mikoshi procession** is said to **commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)**, with participants **parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire**.
Additionally, **Tezutsu Fireworks** are believed to have begun in **Eiroku 1 (1558)** when **{{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元}}’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine**. A stone monument **"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)** stands within the shrine grounds. The **Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)** are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they **parade them like mikoshi**.

* **First Day (Friday)** * **Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)**: From 18:00 * **Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)**: From 18:45

[[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|Toyohashi Gion Festival – Fireworks Display]] '''Toyohashi Gion Festival''' (豊橋祇園祭) is a festival held every **mid-July** at {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)}} in **Sekiwa Town (関屋町), Toyohashi City (豊橋市)**.
The festival centers around the **Mikoshi Togyo (神輿渡御, portable shrine procession)**. Additionally, the **Tezutsu Fireworks (手筒花火)** on the night before the festival and the **fireworks display** on the eve of the main event are well known.
== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by {{ill|Utagawa Hiroshige|ja|歌川広重}}]] The festival **originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)**. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript **"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)**, authored by **{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨}}**, which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The **mikoshi procession** is said to **commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)**, with participants **parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire**.
Additionally, **Tezutsu Fireworks** are believed to have begun in **Eiroku 1 (1558)** when **{{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元}}’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine**. A stone monument **"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)** stands within the shrine grounds. The **Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)** are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they **parade them like mikoshi**.

* **First Day (Friday)** * **Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)**: From 18:00 * **Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)**: From 18:45
* **Second Day (Saturday)** * **Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)**: From 18:00
* **Final Day (Sunday)** * **Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)**: From 10:00 – Includes the **Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)** and **Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance** * **Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)**: From 17:00

* **First Day (Friday)** ** **Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)**: From 18:00 ** **Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)**: From 18:45
* **Second Day (Saturday)** ** **Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)**: From 18:00
* **Final Day (Sunday)** ** **Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)**: From 10:00 – Includes the **Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)** and **Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance** ** **Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)**: From 17:00

[[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|Toyohashi Gion Festival – Fireworks Display]] '''Toyohashi Gion Festival''' (豊橋祇園祭) is a festival held every **mid-July** at {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)}} in **Sekiwa Town (関屋町), Toyohashi City (豊橋市)**.
The festival centers around the **Mikoshi Togyo (神輿渡御, portable shrine procession)**. Additionally, the **Tezutsu Fireworks (手筒花火)** on the night before the festival and the **fireworks display** on the eve of the main event are well known.
== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by {{ill|Utagawa Hiroshige|ja|歌川広重}}]] The festival **originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)**. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript **"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)**, authored by **{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨}}**, which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The **mikoshi procession** is said to **commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)**, with participants **parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire**.
Additionally, **Tezutsu Fireworks** are believed to have begun in **Eiroku 1 (1558)** when **{{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元}}’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine**. A stone monument **"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)** stands within the shrine grounds. The **Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)** are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they **parade them like mikoshi**.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * **Day Before (Thursday)** ** Children's **Sasa Dance (笹踊り)**: 15:00–17:00
* **First Day (Friday)** ** **Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)**: From 18:00 ** **Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)**: From 18:45
* **Second Day (Saturday)** ** **Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)**: From 18:00
* **Final Day (Sunday)** ** **Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)**: From 10:00 – Includes the **Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)** and **Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance** ** **Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)**: From 17:00
== See Also == * {{ill|Gion Festival|ja|祇園祭}} * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)

* **Final Day (Sunday)** ** **Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)**: From 10:00 – Includes the **Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)** and **Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance** ** **Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)**: From 17:00 == See Also == * {{ill|Gion Festival|ja|祇園祭}} * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)}}

[[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|Toyohashi Gion Festival – Fireworks Display]] '''Toyohashi Gion Festival''' (豊橋祇園祭) is a festival held every **mid-July** at {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)}} in **Sekiwa Town (関屋町), Toyohashi City (豊橋市)**.
The festival centers around the **Mikoshi Togyo (神輿渡御, portable shrine procession)**. Additionally, the **Tezutsu Fireworks (手筒花火)** on the night before the festival and the **fireworks display** on the eve of the main event are well known.
== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by {{ill|Utagawa Hiroshige|ja|歌川広重}}]] The festival **originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)**. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript **"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)**, authored by **{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨}}**, which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The **mikoshi procession** is said to **commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)**, with participants **parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire**.
Additionally, **Tezutsu Fireworks** are believed to have begun in **Eiroku 1 (1558)** when **{{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元}}’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine**. A stone monument **"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)** stands within the shrine grounds. The **Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)** are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they **parade them like mikoshi**.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * **Day Before (Thursday)** ** Children's **Sasa Dance (笹踊り)**: 15:00–17:00
* **First Day (Friday)** ** **Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)**: From 18:00 ** **Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)**: From 18:45
* **Second Day (Saturday)** ** **Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)**: From 18:00
* **Final Day (Sunday)** ** **Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)**: From 10:00 – Includes the **Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)** and **Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance** ** **Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)**: From 17:00 == See Also == * {{ill|Gion Festival|ja|祇園祭}} * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)

* **First Day (Friday)** ** **Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)**: From 18:00 ** **Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)**: From 18:45
* **Second Day (Saturday)** ** **Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)**: From 18:00
* **Final Day (Sunday)** ** **Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)**: From 10:00 – Includes the **Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)** and **Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance** ** **Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)**: From 17:00 == See Also == * {{ill|Gion Festival|ja|祇園祭}} * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)}}

revision.text:
[[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|Toyohashi Gion Festival – Fireworks Display]] '''Toyohashi Gion Festival''' (豊橋祇園祭) is a festival held every **mid-July** at {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)}} in **Sekiwa Town (関屋町), Toyohashi City (豊橋市)**.
The festival centers around the **Mikoshi Togyo (神輿渡御, portable shrine procession)**. Additionally, the **Tezutsu Fireworks (手筒花火)** on the night before the festival and the **fireworks display** on the eve of the main event are well known.
== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by {{ill|Utagawa Hiroshige|ja|歌川広重}}]] The festival **originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)**. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript **"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)**, authored by **{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨}}**, which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The **mikoshi procession** is said to **commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)**, with participants **parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire**.
Additionally, **Tezutsu Fireworks** are believed to have begun in **Eiroku 1 (1558)** when **{{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元}}’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine**. A stone monument **"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)** stands within the shrine grounds. The **Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)** are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they **parade them like mikoshi**.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * **Day Before (Thursday)** ** Children's **Sasa Dance (笹踊り)**: 15:00–17:00
* **First Day (Friday)** ** **Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)**: From 18:00 ** **Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)**: From 18:45
* **Second Day (Saturday)** ** **Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)**: From 18:00
* **Final Day (Sunday)** ** **Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)**: From 10:00 – Includes the **Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)** and **Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance** ** **Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)**: From 17:00 == See Also == * {{ill|Gion Festival|ja|祇園祭}} * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)
{{Event-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}}
<!--Toyohashi Gion Festival-->

32 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:豊橋祇園祭 up to rev 90444037
revision.text:
[[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|豊橋祇園祭・打ち上げ花火]] '''豊橋祇園祭'''（とよはしぎおんまつり）は、[[豊橋市]][[関屋町]]の[[吉田神社 (豊橋市)|吉田神社]]で毎年7月中旬に行われる祭事である。
[[神輿渡御]]を本祭とする。前々夜の[[手筒花火]]、前夜の打ち上げ[[花火]]も有名である。
==由来・歴史== [[ファイル:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|サムネイル|天王祭。[[歌川広重]]作]] [[江戸時代]]に天王祭として行われ、[[山本貞晨]]著作『三河国吉田名跡綜録』（吉田神社所蔵）という絵文書にもその様子が描かれている。神輿渡御の行列は[[源頼朝]]をしのぶものとされ、[[鎌倉時代|鎌倉]]・[[平安時代]]の服装で練り歩く。
なお手筒花火は[[永禄]]元年（[[1558年]]）に[[今川義元]]の[[吉田城 (三河国)|吉田城]]代、[[小原鎮実|大原備前守]]の吉田神社への奉納に始まるとされている。吉田神社内に「伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地」の碑がある。大筒花火は町内会が自分で手作り、それを神輿がわりに担いで練り歩く。
==日程== [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|吉田天王社祭礼の図]]*前日（木曜日） **子供笹踊り：15〜17時 *初日（金曜日） **宵祭り：18時より **神前花火奉納（手筒花火）：18時45分より *二日目（土曜日） **前夜祭（打ち上げ花火）：18時より *最終日（日曜日） **例祭式典：10時より[[献幣使]]参向、[[浦安の舞]]奉納 **御幸祭：17時より神輿渡御出発
==関連項目== *[[祇園祭]] *[[吉田神社 (豊橋市)]]
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} *[https://www.toyohashigion.org/ 豊橋祇園祭奉賛会公式サイト] *[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 豊橋の煙火見物] 『旅の小遣帳』時事新報家庭部 編（正和堂書房、1930）
{{Event-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=愛知県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよはしきおんまつり}} [[Category:愛知県の祭り]] [[Category:愛知県の花火大会]] [[Category:豊橋市の文化]] [[Category:三河国]] [[Category:祇園祭・天王祭]]

revision.text:
[[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|豊橋祇園祭・打ち上げ花火]] '''豊橋祇園祭'''（とよはしぎおんまつり）は、[[豊橋市]][[関屋町]]の[[吉田神社 (豊橋市)|吉田神社]]で毎年7月中旬に行われる祭事である。
[[神輿渡御]]を本祭とする。前々夜の[[手筒花火]]、前夜の打ち上げ[[花火]]も有名である。
==由来・歴史== [[ファイル:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|サムネイル|天王祭。[[歌川広重]]作]] [[江戸時代]]に天王祭として行われ、[[山本貞晨]]著作『三河国吉田名跡綜録』（吉田神社所蔵）という絵文書にもその様子が描かれている。神輿渡御の行列は[[源頼朝]]をしのぶものとされ、[[鎌倉時代|鎌倉]]・[[平安時代]]の服装で練り歩く。
なお手筒花火は[[永禄]]元年（[[1558年]]）に[[今川義元]]の[[吉田城 (三河国)|吉田城]]代、[[小原鎮実|大原備前守]]の吉田神社への奉納に始まるとされている。吉田神社内に「伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地」の碑がある。大筒花火は町内会が自分で手作り、それを神輿がわりに担いで練り歩く。
==日程== [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|吉田天王社祭礼の図]]*前日（木曜日） **子供笹踊り：15〜17時 *初日（金曜日） **宵祭り：18時より **神前花火奉納（手筒花火）：18時45分より *二日目（土曜日） **前夜祭（打ち上げ花火）：18時より *最終日（日曜日） **例祭式典：10時より[[献幣使]]参向、[[浦安の舞]]奉納 **御幸祭：17時より神輿渡御出発
==関連項目== *[[祇園祭]] *[[吉田神社 (豊橋市)]]
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} *[https://www.toyohashigion.org/ 豊橋祇園祭奉賛会公式サイト] *[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 豊橋の煙火見物] 『旅の小遣帳』時事新報家庭部 編（正和堂書房、1930）
{{Event-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=愛知県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:とよはしきおんまつり}} [[Category:愛知県の祭り]] [[Category:愛知県の花火大会]] [[Category:豊橋市の文化]] [[Category:三河国]] [[Category:祇園祭・天王祭]]

[[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|Toyohashi Gion Festival – Fireworks Display]] '''Toyohashi Gion Festival''' (豊橋祇園祭) is a festival held every '''mid-July''' at {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in '''Sekiwa Town (関屋町), Toyohashi City (豊橋市)'''.
The festival centers around the '''Mikoshi Togyo (神輿渡御, portable shrine procession)'''. Additionally, the '''Tezutsu Fireworks (手筒花火)''' on the night before the festival and the '''fireworks display''' on the eve of the main event are well known.
== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by {{ill|Utagawa Hiroshige|ja|歌川広重|qq=draft|qq=draft}}]] The festival '''originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)'''. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript '''"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)''', authored by '''{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨|qq=draft|qq=draft}}''', which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The '''mikoshi procession''' is said to '''commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)''', with participants '''parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire'''.
Additionally, '''Tezutsu Fireworks''' are believed to have begun in '''Eiroku 1 (1558)''' when '''{{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine'''. A stone monument '''"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)''' stands within the shrine grounds. The '''Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)''' are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they '''parade them like mikoshi'''.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * '''Day Before (Thursday)''' ''' Children's '''Sasa Dance (笹踊り)''': 15:00–17:00
* '''First Day (Friday)''' ''' '''Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)''': From 18:00 ''' '''Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)''': From 18:45
* '''Second Day (Saturday)''' ''' '''Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)''': From 18:00
* '''Final Day (Sunday)''' ''' '''Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)''': From 10:00 – Includes the '''Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)''' and '''Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance''' ''' '''Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)''': From 17:00 == See Also == * {{ill|Gion Festival|ja|祇園祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)
{{Event-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}}
{{translated page|ja|豊橋祇園祭|version=90444037|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Additionally, '''Tezutsu Fireworks''' are believed to have begun in '''Eiroku 1 (1558)''' when '''{{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|ja|今川義元|qq=draft|qq=draft}}’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine'''. A stone monument '''"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)''' stands within the shrine grounds. The '''Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)''' are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they '''parade them like mikoshi'''.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * '''Day Before (Thursday)''' ''' Children's '''Sasa Dance (笹踊り)''': 15:00–17:00

* '''Second Day (Saturday)''' ''' '''Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)''': From 18:00

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri]][[ja:豊橋祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11633853]] Wikidata name: Toyohashi Gion Matsuri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|Toyohashi Gion Festival – Fireworks Display]] '''Toyohashi Gion Festival''' (豊橋祇園祭) is a festival held every '''mid-July''' at {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}} in '''Sekiwa Town (関屋町), Toyohashi City (豊橋市)'''.
The festival centers around the '''Mikoshi Togyo (神輿渡御, portable shrine procession)'''. Additionally, the '''Tezutsu Fireworks (手筒花火)''' on the night before the festival and the '''fireworks display''' on the eve of the main event are well known.
== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by [[:en:Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]]] The festival '''originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)'''. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript '''"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)''', authored by '''{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Teishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The '''mikoshi procession''' is said to '''commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)''', with participants '''parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire'''.
Additionally, '''Tezutsu Fireworks''' are believed to have begun in '''Eiroku 1 (1558)''' when '''[[:en:Imagawa Yoshimoto|Imagawa Yoshimoto]]’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obara Shigetane|1=Obara Shigetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459277}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine'''. A stone monument '''"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)''' stands within the shrine grounds. The '''Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)''' are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they '''parade them like mikoshi'''.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * '''Day Before (Thursday)''' ''' Children's '''Sasa Dance (笹踊り)''': 15:00–17:00
* '''First Day (Friday)''' ''' '''Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)''': From 18:00 ''' '''Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)''': From 18:45
* '''Second Day (Saturday)''' ''' '''Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)''': From 18:00
* '''Final Day (Sunday)''' ''' '''Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)''': From 10:00 – Includes the '''Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)''' and '''Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance''' ''' '''Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)''': From 17:00 == See Also == * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)

* '''First Day (Friday)''' ''' '''Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)''': From 18:00 ''' '''Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)''': From 18:45
* '''Second Day (Saturday)''' ''' '''Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)''': From 18:00

== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri]][[ja:豊橋祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11633853]] Wikidata name: Toyohashi Gion Matsuri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|Toyohashi Gion Festival – Fireworks Display]] '''Toyohashi Gion Festival''' (豊橋祇園祭) is a festival held every '''mid-July''' at {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}} in '''Sekiwa Town (関屋町), Toyohashi City (豊橋市)'''.
The festival centers around the '''Mikoshi Togyo (神輿渡御, portable shrine procession)'''. Additionally, the '''Tezutsu Fireworks (手筒花火)''' on the night before the festival and the '''fireworks display''' on the eve of the main event are well known.
== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by [[:en:Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]]] The festival '''originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)'''. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript '''"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)''', authored by '''{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Teishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The '''mikoshi procession''' is said to '''commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)''', with participants '''parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire'''.
Additionally, '''Tezutsu Fireworks''' are believed to have begun in '''Eiroku 1 (1558)''' when '''[[:en:Imagawa Yoshimoto|Imagawa Yoshimoto]]’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obara Shigetane|1=Obara Shigetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459277}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine'''. A stone monument '''"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)''' stands within the shrine grounds. The '''Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)''' are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they '''parade them like mikoshi'''.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * '''Day Before (Thursday)''' ''' Children's '''Sasa Dance (笹踊り)''': 15:00–17:00
* '''First Day (Friday)''' ''' '''Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)''': From 18:00 ''' '''Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)''': From 18:45
* '''Second Day (Saturday)''' ''' '''Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)''': From 18:00
* '''Final Day (Sunday)''' ''' '''Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)''': From 10:00 – Includes the '''Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)''' and '''Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance''' ''' '''Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)''': From 17:00 == See Also == * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)
{{Event-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}}
{{translated page|ja|豊橋祇園祭|version=90444037|comment=Imported full ja history}}

* '''First Day (Friday)''' ''' '''Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)''': From 18:00 ''' '''Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)''': From 18:45

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri]][[ja:豊橋祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11633853]] Wikidata name: Toyohashi Gion Matsuri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|Toyohashi Gion Festival – Fireworks Display]] '''Toyohashi Gion Festival''' (豊橋祇園祭) is a festival held every '''mid-July''' at {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}} in '''Sekiwa Town (関屋町), Toyohashi City (豊橋市)'''.
The festival centers around the '''Mikoshi Togyo (神輿渡御, portable shrine procession)'''. Additionally, the '''Tezutsu Fireworks (手筒花火)''' on the night before the festival and the '''fireworks display''' on the eve of the main event are well known.
== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by [[:en:Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]]] The festival '''originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)'''. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript '''"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)''', authored by '''{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Teishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The '''mikoshi procession''' is said to '''commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)''', with participants '''parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire'''.
Additionally, '''Tezutsu Fireworks''' are believed to have begun in '''Eiroku 1 (1558)''' when '''[[:en:Imagawa Yoshimoto|Imagawa Yoshimoto]]’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obara Shigetane|1=Obara Shigetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459277}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine'''. A stone monument '''"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)''' stands within the shrine grounds. The '''Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)''' are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they '''parade them like mikoshi'''.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * '''Day Before (Thursday)''' ''' Children's '''Sasa Dance (笹踊り)''': 15:00–17:00
* '''First Day (Friday)''' ''' '''Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)''': From 18:00 ''' '''Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)''': From 18:45
* '''Second Day (Saturday)''' ''' '''Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)''': From 18:00
* '''Final Day (Sunday)''' ''' '''Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)''': From 10:00 – Includes the '''Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)''' and '''Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance''' ''' '''Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)''': From 17:00 == See Also == * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)
{{Event-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}}
{{translated page|ja|豊橋祇園祭|version=90444037|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Additionally, '''Tezutsu Fireworks''' are believed to have begun in '''Eiroku 1 (1558)''' when '''[[:en:Imagawa Yoshimoto|Imagawa Yoshimoto]]’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obara Shigetane|1=Obara Shigetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459277}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine'''. * '''First Day (Friday)''' ''' '''Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)''': From 18:00 ''' '''Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)''': From 18:45
* '''Second Day (Saturday)''' ''' '''Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)''': From 18:00

== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri]][[ja:豊橋祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri]][[ja:豊橋祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri]][[ja:豊橋祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11633853]] Wikidata name: Toyohashi Gion Matsuri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|Toyohashi Gion Festival – Fireworks Display]] '''Toyohashi Gion Festival''' (豊橋祇園祭) is a festival held every '''mid-July''' at {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}} in '''Sekiwa Town (関屋町), Toyohashi City (豊橋市)'''.
The festival centers around the '''Mikoshi Togyo (神輿渡御, portable shrine procession)'''. Additionally, the '''Tezutsu Fireworks (手筒花火)''' on the night before the festival and the '''fireworks display''' on the eve of the main event are well known.
== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by [[:en:Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]]] The festival '''originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)'''. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript '''"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)''', authored by '''{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Teishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The '''mikoshi procession''' is said to '''commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)''', with participants '''parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire'''.
Additionally, '''Tezutsu Fireworks''' are believed to have begun in '''Eiroku 1 (1558)''' when '''[[:en:Imagawa Yoshimoto|Imagawa Yoshimoto]]’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obara Shigetane|1=Obara Shigetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459277}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine'''. A stone monument '''"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)''' stands within the shrine grounds. The '''Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)''' are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they '''parade them like mikoshi'''.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * '''Day Before (Thursday)''' ''' Children's '''Sasa Dance (笹踊り)''': 15:00–17:00
* '''First Day (Friday)''' ''' '''Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)''': From 18:00 ''' '''Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)''': From 18:45
* '''Second Day (Saturday)''' ''' '''Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)''': From 18:00
* '''Final Day (Sunday)''' ''' '''Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)''': From 10:00 – Includes the '''Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)''' and '''Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance''' ''' '''Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)''': From 17:00 == See Also == * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)

The festival centers around the '''Mikoshi Togyo (神輿渡御, portable shrine procession)'''. Additionally, the '''Tezutsu Fireworks (手筒花火)''' on the night before the festival and the '''fireworks display''' on the eve of the main event are well known.
== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by [[:en:Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]]] The festival '''originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)'''. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript '''"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)''', authored by '''{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Teishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The '''mikoshi procession''' is said to '''commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)''', with participants '''parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire'''.
Additionally, '''Tezutsu Fireworks''' are believed to have begun in '''Eiroku 1 (1558)''' when '''[[:en:Imagawa Yoshimoto|Imagawa Yoshimoto]]’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obara Shigetane|1=Obara Shigetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459277}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine'''. A stone monument '''"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)''' stands within the shrine grounds. The '''Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)''' are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they '''parade them like mikoshi'''.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * '''Day Before (Thursday)''' ''' Children's '''Sasa Dance (笹踊り)''': 15:00–17:00
* '''First Day (Friday)''' ''' '''Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)''': From 18:00 ''' '''Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)''': From 18:45
* '''Second Day (Saturday)''' ''' '''Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)''': From 18:00
* '''Final Day (Sunday)''' ''' '''Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)''': From 10:00 – Includes the '''Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)''' and '''Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance''' ''' '''Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)''': From 17:00 == See Also == * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri]][[ja:豊橋祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri]][[ja:豊橋祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri]][[ja:豊橋祇園祭]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11633853]] Wikidata name: Toyohashi Gion Matsuri [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|Toyohashi Gion Festival – Fireworks Display]] '''Toyohashi Gion Festival''' (豊橋祇園祭) is a festival held every '''mid-July''' at {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}} in '''Sekiwa Town (関屋町), Toyohashi City (豊橋市)'''.
The festival centers around the '''Mikoshi Togyo (神輿渡御, portable shrine procession)'''. Additionally, the '''Tezutsu Fireworks (手筒花火)''' on the night before the festival and the '''fireworks display''' on the eve of the main event are well known.
== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by [[:en:Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]]] The festival '''originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)'''. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript '''"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)''', authored by '''{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Teishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The '''mikoshi procession''' is said to '''commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)''', with participants '''parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire'''.
Additionally, '''Tezutsu Fireworks''' are believed to have begun in '''Eiroku 1 (1558)''' when '''[[:en:Imagawa Yoshimoto|Imagawa Yoshimoto]]’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obara Shigetane|1=Obara Shigetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459277}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine'''. A stone monument '''"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)''' stands within the shrine grounds. The '''Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)''' are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they '''parade them like mikoshi'''.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * '''Day Before (Thursday)''' ''' Children's '''Sasa Dance (笹踊り)''': 15:00–17:00
* '''First Day (Friday)''' ''' '''Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)''': From 18:00 ''' '''Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)''': From 18:45
* '''Second Day (Saturday)''' ''' '''Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)''': From 18:00
* '''Final Day (Sunday)''' ''' '''Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)''': From 10:00 – Includes the '''Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)''' and '''Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance''' ''' '''Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)''': From 17:00 == See Also == * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)
{{Event-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}}
{{translated page|ja|豊橋祇園祭|version=90444037|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:豊橋祇園祭]] [[ja:%E8%B1%8A%E6%A9%8B%E7%A5%87%E5%9C%92%E7%A5%AD]]

== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by [[:en:Hiroshige|Utagawa Hiroshige]]]] The festival '''originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)'''. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript '''"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)''', authored by '''{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Teishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The '''mikoshi procession''' is said to '''commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)''', with participants '''parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire'''.
Additionally, '''Tezutsu Fireworks''' are believed to have begun in '''Eiroku 1 (1558)''' when '''[[:en:Imagawa Yoshimoto|Imagawa Yoshimoto]]’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obara Shigetane|1=Obara Shigetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459277}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine'''. A stone monument '''"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)''' stands within the shrine grounds. The '''Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)''' are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they '''parade them like mikoshi'''.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * '''Day Before (Thursday)''' ''' Children's '''Sasa Dance (笹踊り)''': 15:00–17:00
* '''First Day (Friday)''' ''' '''Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)''': From 18:00 ''' '''Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)''': From 18:45
* '''Second Day (Saturday)''' ''' '''Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)''': From 18:00
* '''Final Day (Sunday)''' ''' '''Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)''': From 10:00 – Includes the '''Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)''' and '''Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance''' ''' '''Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)''': From 17:00 == See Also == * [[:en:Gion Matsuri|Gion Festival]] * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri]][[ja:豊橋祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri]][[ja:豊橋祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri]][[ja:豊橋祇園祭]]
[[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|Toyohashi Gion Festival – Fireworks Display]] '''Toyohashi Gion Festival''' (豊橋祇園祭) is a festival held every '''mid-July''' at {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}} in '''Sekiwa Town (関屋町), Toyohashi City (豊橋市)'''.
The festival centers around the '''Mikoshi Togyo (神輿渡御, portable shrine procession)'''. Additionally, the '''Tezutsu Fireworks (手筒花火)''' on the night before the festival and the '''fireworks display''' on the eve of the main event are well known.
== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by {{ill|Hiroshige|en|Hiroshige|WD=Q200798|lt=Utagawa Hiroshige}}]] The festival '''originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)'''. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript '''"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)''', authored by '''{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Teishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The '''mikoshi procession''' is said to '''commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)''', with participants '''parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire'''.
Additionally, '''Tezutsu Fireworks''' are believed to have begun in '''Eiroku 1 (1558)''' when '''{{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|en|Imagawa Yoshimoto|WD=Q1054305|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto}}’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obara Shigetane|1=Obara Shigetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459277}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine'''. A stone monument '''"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)''' stands within the shrine grounds. The '''Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)''' are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they '''parade them like mikoshi'''.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * '''Day Before (Thursday)''' ''' Children's '''Sasa Dance (笹踊り)''': 15:00–17:00
* '''First Day (Friday)''' ''' '''Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)''': From 18:00 ''' '''Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)''': From 18:45
* '''Second Day (Saturday)''' ''' '''Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)''': From 18:00
* '''Final Day (Sunday)''' ''' '''Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)''': From 10:00 – Includes the '''Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)''' and '''Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance''' ''' '''Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)''': From 17:00 == See Also == * {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival}} * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)
{{Event-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}}
{{translated page|ja|豊橋祇園祭|version=90444037|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * '''Day Before (Thursday)''' ''' Children's '''Sasa Dance (笹踊り)''': 15:00–17:00
* '''First Day (Friday)''' ''' '''Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)''': From 18:00 ''' '''Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)''': From 18:45
* '''Second Day (Saturday)''' ''' '''Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)''': From 18:00
* '''Final Day (Sunday)''' ''' '''Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)''': From 10:00 – Includes the '''Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)''' and '''Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance''' ''' '''Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)''': From 17:00 == See Also == * {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival}} * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)

{{wikidata link|Q11633853}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Culture of Toyohashi]] [[Category:Festivals in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Fireworks festivals in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Gion festivals]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri]][[ja:豊橋祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri]][[ja:豊橋祇園祭]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Toyohashi Gion Matsuri]][[ja:豊橋祇園祭]]
[[File:Toyohahsi Gion Matsuri 02.jpg|thumb|275px|Toyohashi Gion Festival – Fireworks Display]] '''Toyohashi Gion Festival''' (豊橋祇園祭) is a festival held every '''mid-July''' at {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}} in '''Sekiwa Town (関屋町), Toyohashi City (豊橋市)'''.
The festival centers around the '''Mikoshi Togyo (神輿渡御, portable shrine procession)'''. Additionally, the '''Tezutsu Fireworks (手筒花火)''' on the night before the festival and the '''fireworks display''' on the eve of the main event are well known.
== Origin & History == [[File:Yoshida, Tenno Festival on the Fifteenth Day of the Sixth Month LACMA M.66.35.23.jpg|thumb|The Tenno Festival, by {{ill|Hiroshige|en|Hiroshige|WD=Q200798|lt=Utagawa Hiroshige|1=Hiroshige}}]] The festival '''originated in the Edo period as the Tenno Festival (天王祭)'''. It is also depicted in the illustrated manuscript '''"Mikawa no Kuni Yoshida Meiseki Sōroku" (三河国吉田名跡綜録)''', authored by '''{{ill|Yamamoto Teishin|ja|山本貞晨|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamamoto Teishin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''', which is kept at Yoshida Shrine.
The '''mikoshi procession''' is said to '''commemorate Minamoto no Yoritomo (源頼朝)''', with participants '''parading in Kamakura and Heian period attire'''.
Additionally, '''Tezutsu Fireworks''' are believed to have begun in '''Eiroku 1 (1558)''' when '''{{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|en|Imagawa Yoshimoto|WD=Q1054305|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|1=Imagawa Yoshimoto}}’s Yoshida Castle governor, {{ill|Obara Shigetane|ja|小原鎮実|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obara Shigetane|1=Q11459277|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11459277}} (Ōhara Bizen no Kami, 大原備前守), dedicated them to Yoshida Shrine'''. A stone monument '''"Birthplace of the Traditional Mikawa Tezutsu Fireworks" (伝承三河伝統 手筒花火発祥之地)''' stands within the shrine grounds. The '''Ōzutsu Fireworks (大筒花火)''' are handcrafted by local neighborhood associations, and they '''parade them like mikoshi'''.
== Schedule == [[File:吉田天王社祭礼図.jpg|thumb|500px|Illustration of Yoshida Tenno Shrine Festival]] * '''Day Before (Thursday)''' ''' Children's '''Sasa Dance (笹踊り)''': 15:00–17:00
* '''First Day (Friday)''' ''' '''Yoimatsuri (宵祭り, Night Festival)''': From 18:00 ''' '''Shinzen Fireworks Dedication (神前花火奉納, Tezutsu Fireworks)''': From 18:45
* '''Second Day (Saturday)''' ''' '''Zenyasai (前夜祭, Fireworks Display Eve)''': From 18:00
* '''Final Day (Sunday)''' ''' '''Reisai Ceremony (例祭式典)''': From 10:00 – Includes the '''Kenpeishi Sankō (献幣使参向)''' and '''Urayasu no Mai (浦安の舞) dedication dance''' ''' '''Miyuki Festival (御幸祭, Mikoshi Procession)''': From 17:00 == See Also == * {{ill|Gion Matsuri|en|Gion Matsuri|WD=Q979873|lt=Gion Festival|1=Gion Matsuri}} * {{ill|Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|ja|吉田神社 (豊橋市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshida Shrine (Toyohashi)|qq=}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toyohashi Gion Matsuri}} * [https://www.toyohashigion.org/ Official Website of the Toyohashi Gion Festival Sponsorship Association] * [https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1190665/42 Fireworks Viewing in Toyohashi] – From *Tabi no Koguchō* (『旅の小遣帳』), Jiji Shinpo Family Section (1930)
{{Event-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Aichi Prefecture}}
